plan: Exit leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) - they decay and can gap against you.
Trim SPY (30%) to ~20% to cap single-name risk.
Lean on income (QQQI) + broad index; hold a 15-20% cash buffer.
Use tight stops - the goal is small, steady gains and shallow drawdowns.
conservative.read: A ~1.5%/mo steady target is realistic for a de-risked book; expect calmer swings but you give up some upside in strong tapes. Preservation first.

plan: Keep leveraged ETFs small (<=3%) and on tight stops - they decay and amplify drawdowns.
Best fresh setup: ZS: WATCH 8/12, entry 150.57-150.42, stop 150.17, targets 152.86/156.2/157.7, R 5.77. A starter (~4%) only if it triggers.
Hold a ~10% cash buffer and respect every stop.
balanced.read: A ~2.5%/mo target is aggressive but not impossible in a strong tape; hinges on cutting concentration/leverage + consistency. Expect losing months - size so a bad one (~-42.53%) doesn't force your hand.

plan: Lean into leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) toward ~6% each - but hold a STRICT stop; they decay and can drop hard fast.
Press the strongest scanner setup: ZS: WATCH 8/12, entry 150.57-150.42, stop 150.17, targets 152.86/156.2/157.7, R 5.77 (~6%).
Run a small (~5%) cash buffer - stay invested for the upside.
Wider but STRICT stops. This mode can lose a lot fast - discipline is everything.
aggressive.read: A ~4%/mo stretch target needs leverage + a strong tape + tight discipline; the same leverage means a bad month can run past -72%. Higher upside AND higher risk of a deep drawdown - not a promise.

plan: Exit leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) - too volatile for parked cash.
Build QQQI toward ~30% as your monthly-distribution anchor.
Keep the rest in broad index + a cash buffer; the distributions pay you monthly.
Low-drawdown income park - but NAV can still dip and distributions vary (not a savings account).
income.read: Covered-call ETFs (QQQI/JEPQ/JEPI/SPYI) can distribute ~1%/mo (~13%/yr) as steady income, but they cap upside and NAV can fall in a selloff. Good for parking cash for yield, not for growth; distributions are variable, not guaranteed.
